prompt: Japanese Edo-period ukiyo-e woodblock print illustrating gradient descent as a solitary traveler moving down a stylized mountain marked with contour lines indicating elevation. The mountain is shaped into smooth terraced bands, forming a loss landscape that converges into a single valley at the base. Use flat, unmodulated areas of indigo, ochre, and vermillion, with bold black outlines and no photographic shading. Subtle woodgrain texture is visible throughout. Composition inspired by Katsushika Hokusai, with a strong diagonal mountain layout. No text, no numbers. Traditional washi paper texture background.
tradition_approach: Flat unmodulated color areas, bold black outlines, no photographic shading, diagonal compositional structure, visible woodgrain texture, muted indigo-ochre-vermillion palette following Hokusai conventions.
